reforms.description: The government aims to promote innovation by increasing public financial support for R&D, particularly in the private sector, while ensuring that the allocation of funds is efficient and generates additional research activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D is expected to stimulate private sector investment in innovation, leading to enhanced productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set ambitious goals to promote innovation and has allocated public support to innovation through various schemes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the government's approach to fostering innovation, with potential long-term impacts on the economy through increased R&D spending.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set ambitious goals to promote innovation, hoping to raise the rate of R&D spending, especially in the private sector.

reforms.description: The government recognizes the need to improve framework conditions for innovation by strengthening competition policy and relaxing product market regulations to encourage firms to innovate.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing competition and reducing regulatory barriers can stimulate innovation by encouraging firms to invest in new technologies and processes.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities want to ensure that the benefits of innovation spread further by improving framework conditions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is crucial for creating a conducive environment for innovation, which is essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: If the authorities want to ensure that the benefits of innovation spread further, they should continue to improve framework conditions.

reforms.description: The public research sector aims to strengthen private-public research links, which may require new mechanisms to facilitate the commercialization of university innovations, including allowing public-sector employees to work in the private sector without financial risks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between public research and private firms can lead to increased innovation and commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for additional mechanisms to facilitate commercialization was highlighted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving the commercialization of research, which is crucial for enhancing innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Private-public research links should be strengthened further, which could require additional mechanisms facilitating the commercialisation of university innovations.

reforms.description: The R&D funding programmes should be de-linked from regional policy to enhance their effectiveness and focus on innovation.
reforms.component_name: De-linking R&D Funding from Regional Policy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing R&D funding on innovation rather than regional policy, resources can be allocated more effectively to drive technological advancements.
reforms.status_evidence: The need to de-link R&D funding from regional policy was emphasized.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the allocation of R&D resources, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The R&D funding programmes should also be clearly de-linked from the regional policy.

reforms.description: The Research Council of Norway (RCN) was restructured to enhance its role in funding and supporting innovation activities, with a clear mandate to cooperate with other institutions like Innovation Norway and SIVA.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the institutional framework for innovation support, the reform aims to stimulate private-sector R&D and enhance overall innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The RCN has been operational with its new structure and mandate.
reforms.importance_rationale: The restructuring of the RCN represents a significant overhaul of the innovation support system, aiming to enhance collaboration and effectiveness in funding R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The intention of the reforms is that each institution should have clear and distinct mandates, and that they should cooperate with each other to provide a better service to the private sector.

reforms.description: The Skattefunn tax credit scheme was introduced to incentivize private sector R&D by providing a 20% deduction on R&D costs, significantly lowering the financial burden on firms engaging in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the cost of R&D, the Skattefunn scheme encourages firms to invest in innovative activities, potentially leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Skattefunn has been operational since its introduction and is popular among businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Skattefunn tax credit is a significant tool for stimulating private R&D investment, although it is part of a broader set of innovation policies.
reforms.source_quote: The Skattefunn provides a 20% deduction of R&D costs up to NOK4million per firm and per year for internal projects.

reforms.description: The FORNY umbrella programme was established to facilitate the commercialization of research results from Norwegian universities, providing support for technology-transfer offices and advising on new company startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the commercialization of research, the programme aims to translate academic findings into marketable innovations, thereby boosting economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: Technology-transfer offices have been set up in Norwegian universities as part of the FORNY programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the FORNY programme represents a significant effort to enhance the commercialization of research, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Technology-transfer offices staffed by 10-15 persons have been set up in Norwegian universities as from 2003 (the FORNY umbrella programme, with a 2006 budget of €15 million facilitates this, and other ways of commercialising the results of public research).

reforms.description: The Nationwide seed capital scheme was introduced to stimulate private investment in start-ups by providing state incentives and facilitating access to funding for new firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing seed capital, the scheme aims to lower the barriers for new firms to enter the market, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme is not yet operational, reflecting investor resistance to restrictions on follow-on investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the seed capital scheme is a significant step towards addressing funding gaps for start-ups, which is critical for enhancing the entrepreneurial ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: A further policy response to this market gap in funding for new firms has been to set up private/public seed capital funds to stimulate investors via state incentives.

reforms.description: Norway aims to raise private R&D spending by more than a percentage point of GDP over the next few years, reflecting a strong political commitment to enhance innovation activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, which are crucial for sustaining economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The current ambitious policy objective is to raise private R&D spending by more than a percentage point of GDP over the next few years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy shift aimed at addressing low innovation activity in the private sector, which is critical for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The current ambitious policy objective is to raise private R&D spending by more than a percentage point of GDP over the next few years.

reforms.description: The FORNY programme provides long-term financing for qualified research and innovation centres, facilitating cooperation between academia and the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration between academia and industry, the programme aims to increase innovation and R&D spending, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is facilitating cooperation on research between academia and the business sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to strengthen public-private research links, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in Norway.
reforms.source_quote: co-operation on research between academia and the business sector is now facilitated by long-term financing of qualified research and innovation centres under the FORNY programme.

reforms.description: Norway aims to increase R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2010, with a target of 2% from the private sector and 1% from the public sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy intention is to increase R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: This ambitious target represents a structural change in Norway's approach to R&D funding, aiming to significantly boost innovation and economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: the policy intention is to increase R&D spending to 3% of GDP (2% private, 1% public) by 2010.

reforms.description: The 'Norwegian Centres of Excellence' initiative aims to provide stable funding for high-quality research groups, prioritizing mathematics, science, and technology research, while also increasing the number of PhD and post-doc positions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the quality and quantity of skilled researchers, the initiative is expected to boost innovation capacity and productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: Current and recent policy initiatives aim to strengthen the numbers and quality of people in the relevant disciplines.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in funding high-quality research, which is crucial for enhancing Norway's innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Norwegian Centres of Excellence' will secure stable funding for research groups deemed to be of high quality.

reforms.description: Increased financial support will be provided to Norwegian researchers seeking research experience abroad, enhancing their skills and international collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating international experience, the reform aims to enhance the skills and networks of researchers, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Increased financial support will be given to Norwegian researchers seeking research experience abroad.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it enhances the capabilities of researchers, contributing to the overall innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Increased financial support will be given to Norwegian researchers seeking research experience abroad.
